_comment: Global market assumptions shared across ALL profiles. No withdrawal sequences here — those are per-profile in economic.json.

overrides._comment: Years 1-5: conservative rebalancing, standard cash reserve
Years 6-15: slightly relaxed capgain limit as brokerage grows
Years 16-30: reduced cash reserve months, higher capgain limit

readme.purpose: Global market behavior settings shared across ALL profiles. Overrides section lets you change these per year-range. Profile economic.json is merged on top for profile-specific overrides.

bad_market.drawdown_threshold: Portfolio decline fraction that triggers 'bad market' mode. E.g. 0.15 = 15% below peak triggers bad-market withdrawal sequencing and rebalancing suppression.
bad_market.use_recession_flag: true = also trigger bad-market mode if macroeconomic recession flag is set in the stochastic model, even if drawdown threshold not yet hit.

cash_reserve.months: Months of withdrawal target kept as cash buffer. E.g. 6 = half-year buffer always maintained.
cash_reserve.allow_dip_below_reserve: false = never withdraw below cash floor even if it means falling short of withdrawal target.
cash_reserve.use_cash_only_in_bad_markets: true = only draw from cash reserve during bad markets; use investment accounts otherwise.

rebalancing.global_drift_threshold: Maximum allowed class drift as fraction of target before rebalancing triggers. E.g. 0.12 = 12% drift band (±12% of target weight). Overrides the per-class ±10% default in allocation_yearly.json if set here.
rebalancing.require_cash_above_reserve_to_rebalance: true = do not rebalance if cash is below reserve threshold.
rebalancing.suppress_in_bad_market: true = pause rebalancing entirely during bad-market periods to avoid locking in losses.
rebalancing.brokerage_capgain_limit_k: Maximum realized capital gains in $k per year from brokerage rebalancing. Prevents large unexpected tax bills from a single rebalance.
rebalancing.brokerage_enabled: true/false. Master switch for brokerage rebalancing. TRAD/Roth always rebalance costlessly.

withdrawals.shock_scaling_enabled: true = scale down withdrawals during bad markets. false = always withdraw full target regardless of market.
withdrawals.drawdown_threshold: Portfolio drawdown fraction at which scaling begins. Should match bad_market.drawdown_threshold.
withdrawals.min_scaling_factor: Minimum fraction of target withdrawal allowed. E.g. 0.65 = never withdraw less than 65% of target. Works with floor_k in withdrawal_schedule.json.

scale_curve_options.linear: Withdrawal scales linearly from 100% at threshold to min_scaling_factor at full drawdown.
scale_curve_options.poly: Polynomial scaling. scale_poly_alpha > 1 = gentler at first, sharper near bottom.
scale_curve_options.exp: Exponential scaling. scale_exp_lambda controls decay rate.

withdrawals.use_recession_flag: Same as bad_market — also scale withdrawals if recession flag is set.
withdrawals.makeup_enabled: true = in good years following a scaled-down year, make up missed withdrawals.
withdrawals.makeup_ratio: Fraction of missed amount to recover per good year. E.g. 0.3 = recover 30% of the deficit each year.
withdrawals.makeup_cap_per_year: Maximum makeup as fraction of annual target. E.g. 0.1 = makeup never exceeds 10% of that year's target.

readme.overrides_note: Time-bounded only. Each override applies for its years range then reverts to defaults above. No permanent overrides — change the defaults block instead.
